_readme: This file is the single source of truth for both portfolio pages. Edit it at /edit/ in your browser, or edit it here directly. Text fields support **double asterisks** to highlight key terms.

profile.name: Md Abu Sayeb Rayhan
profile.roleResearch: Robot perception and embodied AI, built on computer vision and generative modelling
profile.summaryIndustry: I build computer-vision and language systems that run in production, not just in notebooks. Over eighteen months at **Othello Financial Services** in **Japan** I took real-time video analytics from prototype to deployment on Azure, cutting inference latency by **70%** and production costs by **50%**. Before that I led a three-person team that delivered a **multilingual OCR and retrieval system** for a Bangladesh government project, from first design through to release. The work I enjoy most is the kind where a model has to hold up against messy data, a tight latency budget and real users.
profile.summaryResearch: I am a computer-vision researcher with three peer-reviewed papers and eighteen months of industry experience deploying perception systems at scale. My work sits where **visual perception**, **generative modelling** and **language understanding** meet, and I am looking for a research-based Master's position where I can pursue that intersection properly.
profile.availabilityIndustry: Open to AI Engineer and Data Scientist roles
profile.availabilityResearch: Seeking research-based Master's positions for the 2026-27 intake
profile.roleResearchSub: Robot perception & embodied AI · Multi-object tracking, activity recognition & pose estimation · Multimodal vision-language models · Generative models for simulation

news.text: Submitted **RGEF: Reliability-Gated Evidential Fusion for Robust Medical AI** to the IEEE International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-Intelligence and Internet-of-Things (**RAAICON 2026**).
Looking for **research-based Master's positions** for the 2026-27 intake.
Presented two papers at **IEEE QPAIN 2026** and received the **Best Presenter Award**.
Finished eighteen months as a Data Scientist at **Othello Financial Services** in **Japan** and returned to Dhaka.
Joined **Othello Financial Services** in **Japan** as a **Data Scientist** on the AGiFT AI Camera team.
Graduated from **Comilla University** with a BSc in Computer Science and Engineering.
Joined a **Bangladesh government project** as an AI Engineer, leading a three-person team until July 2024.
Began working as a **Research Assistant** at Comilla University with Partha Chakraborty, on human-robot interaction and OCR.

metrics.label: Inference latency reduced in a real-time analytics pipeline
Production cost cut through automated video generation
Detection accuracy on custom-trained YOLO models
Peer-reviewed papers published, plus one under review

researchStatement: My work asks whether a vision model can still be trusted once conditions stop matching its training data. The same question runs through all four of my papers: what survives when the **generator changes**, when the clinic has **no specialist**, when the farm has **no GPU**, and when the model **should not be answering at all**.

In synthetic image detection I looked for **forensic invariants** that persist across generators rather than artefacts a classifier memorises. The rice leaf study asked the same of architecture under real compute budgets, and BD Eye-Doc under real clinical access. RGEF turned the question inward, using **evidential reliability gating** so a model can abstain instead of guess. Eighteen months at Othello gave me the practical half: tracking and activity recognition on live video, where occlusion, drift and a fixed latency budget show you exactly where models degrade quietly.

**Embodied AI is where I want to take this next.** An agent acting in the world meets all of these failures at once and cannot pause to ask for help, which makes reliability a control problem rather than a reporting one. I am looking for a research-based Master's where I can work on that directly.

researchAreas.title: Tracking, activity recognition & pose estimation
researchAreas.desc: Multi-object tracking, human activity recognition and pose estimation on live video. Built and shipped in production at Othello (ByteTrack, MoveNet, MMAction2), and the same perceptual problem underlies the rice leaf and BD Eye-Doc classification studies.

researchAreas.title: Multimodal vision-language models
researchAreas.desc: Coupling vision with language so a system can act on and explain what it sees. Evidenced in BD Eye-Doc's conversational screening interface and the retrieval-augmented pipelines I built at Othello and on the Bangladesh government project.

researchAreas.title: Generative models for simulation & robotic learning
researchAreas.desc: Using generative models to produce data and scenarios that cannot be collected directly. Rooted in my thesis on diffusion models, and turned toward detection in the forensic invariant learning paper, which asks what generative signal survives across models.

researchAreas.title: Robot perception & embodied AI
researchAreas.desc: Perception built for systems that act in the world, not just classify it. This is the direction I want to move toward next, building on the abstention and reliability-gating work in RGEF.

publications.title: Forensic Invariant Learning for Synthetic Image Detection: Bridging Benford's Law and Topological Analysis with Machine Learning Ensembles
publications.authors: **Md Abu Sayeb Rayhan**, Ajoy Shutradhar, Hustagna Hiya
publications.venue: IEEE International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence and Networking (QPAIN) 2026
publications.note: Detecting AI-generated imagery through **physics-informed invariants** rather than learned artefacts. It combines Benford's Law digit statistics with topological features across a six-model ensemble, targeting cues that survive a change of generator. That is the failure mode most detectors share.

publications.title: BD Eye-Doc: When Computer Vision Meets Conversational AI for Scalable Eye Disease Screening in Bangladesh
publications.authors: Ajoy Shutradhar, **Md Abu Sayeb Rayhan**, Mahmuda Khatun, Bishwajit Das
publications.venue: IEEE International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence and Networking (QPAIN) 2026
publications.note: An image-classification pipeline paired with a conversational interface, so screening can reach places where ophthalmologists are scarce. The hard question here is **deployability in low-resource clinical settings** rather than raw accuracy.

publications.title: Is CNN Dead? A Comprehensive Empirical Analysis of Convolutional Neural Networks, Ensemble Methods, and Vision Transformers for Rice Leaf Disease Classification in Low-Resource Agricultural Settings
publications.venue: International Conference on Emerging Computing and Communication Technologies (ECCT) 2026 · Advances in Computer Science Research, Atlantis Press (Springer Nature)
publications.note: A controlled comparison of CNNs, ensembles and **Vision Transformers** under the compute and data constraints that agricultural deployment actually imposes. The strongest architecture on ImageNet is rarely the right choice there.

publications.title: RGEF: Reliability-Gated Evidential Fusion for Robust Medical AI
publications.venue: IEEE International Conference on Robotics, Automation, Artificial-Intelligence and Internet-of-Things (RAAICON) 2026
publications.note: Evidential deep learning for medical decision support. **Reliability gating** weights each modality by how trustworthy its evidence is, so the model can abstain rather than guess when its inputs are unreliable.

talks.title: Forensic Invariant Learning for Synthetic Image Detection
BD Eye-Doc: Computer Vision Meets Conversational AI for Eye Disease Screening
Is CNN Dead? CNNs, Ensembles and Vision Transformers for Rice Leaf Disease Classification

bullets: Built **multi-object tracking** (ByteTrack), **pose estimation** (MoveNet) and **human activity recognition** (MMAction2) pipelines for automated behaviour analysis on live retail video.
Designed a spatial clustering pipeline applying **OPTICS** to pose-estimation output for group-dynamics modelling; parallel inference optimisation reduced processing latency by **~70%**.
Developed a video **event-segmentation** system on a fine-tuned YOLO detector at **90%+ accuracy**, automating highlight extraction and cutting production cost by **50%**.
Implemented a **retrieval-augmented generation** system (LangChain, Pinecone, GPT-4) for context-aware document querying, deployed on **Microsoft Azure**.
Integrated IoT edge devices feeding real-time computer vision pipelines for automated data acquisition.
Developed a multilingual (Bangla/English) **NLP and OCR** retrieval pipeline spanning PDF, Word, Excel and image formats, combining optical character recognition with **dense embeddings**.
Shipped as a desktop application built with PyQt and ChromaDB.
**Led a three-person team** across the full product lifecycle, from design through deployment.
Conducted empirical studies in **Human–Robot Interaction** and **Optical Character Recognition**, covering dataset design, experimental protocol and quantitative analysis.
Prepared and validated results subsequently incorporated into **peer-reviewed conference publications**.

projects.title: Universal NFT Generation Platform
projects.desc: Generalised a sports-specific video system into a platform handling user-generated footage across festivals, concerts and other domains. Fine-tuned action detection models and built the pipeline for automated clip creation.

projects.title: Sports Highlight Generation System
projects.desc: Trained a custom object detection model and built a real-time video processing pipeline that identifies key moments in sports footage and generates clips without human intervention.

projects.title: Real-Time In-Store Behaviour Analytics
projects.desc: Real-time system detecting customer groupings and product interest from in-store camera feeds using pose estimation and density-based spatial clustering. Parallelised inference brought latency down sharply.

projects.title: RAG Customer Support Chatbot
projects.desc: End-to-end retrieval-augmented chatbot that retrieves relevant context from a vector index and generates grounded answers. Built and deployed solo on Azure.

projects.title: Context Searcher
projects.desc: RAG-based desktop application for keyword and semantic search across PDF, DOCX, XLSX, TXT and images in Bengali and English, combining OCR with dense embeddings.

projects.title: ADAS Lane & Vehicle Detection
projects.desc: Real-time lane boundary and vehicle detection for driver-assistance systems, combining a YOLOv5 detector fine-tuned on a custom dataset with classical computer vision for lane geometry.

education.coursework: Artificial Intelligence, Algorithm Design, Theory of Computation, Introduction to Data Science, Object-Oriented Programming, Research-Based Project.
education.thesisDesc: Fine-tuned **Stable Diffusion** with a U-Net backbone on a purpose-built dataset of Bangladeshi landmarks. I examined prompt engineering and **conditioning mechanisms** as levers on generative fidelity, and used OpenCV post-processing to reduce artefacts.

futureDirections.q: Can a model's own uncertainty drive its behaviour rather than merely report it?
futureDirections.a: RGEF stops at weighting and abstention. In a sequential or embodied setting the more useful question is whether an agent can use its own evidential state to decide where to look next, when to slow down, or when to ask for help. That turns reliability from a diagnostic into a policy.

futureDirections.q: What perceptual structure survives a change of sensor?
futureDirections.a: The forensic invariant work looked for cues that persist across image generators. The same framing applies to a robot moving between cameras, lighting conditions and environments. I would like to know how far invariant-seeking transfers from detecting synthetic images to maintaining perception across embodiment.

futureDirections.q: How far can generative simulation substitute for data that cannot be collected?
futureDirections.a: Low-resource settings and embodied agents share a data problem, and my thesis work on diffusion models suggests one route around it. The open question is which properties of synthetic data actually transfer, and how to tell before deployment rather than after.
